---
title: "Replication of Mian, Sufi and Verner (2017), Figure I"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 2
---

## 1. Configuration

```{r setup}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(readr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = TRUE,
  fig.width = 12, fig.height = 4.2, dpi = 150
)

# The paper uses 2,000 draws in both stages.
n_boot_bias <- 2000L
n_boot_ci <- 2000L

workers <- 10L

# Iteration settings from the Matlab program.
bias_tol <- 0.0015
bias_step <- 0.5
max_bias_iterations <- 50L

# One BLAS thread per worker prevents CPU oversubscription.
Sys.setenv(
  OMP_NUM_THREADS = 1,
  OPENBLAS_NUM_THREADS = 1,
  MKL_NUM_THREADS = 1,
  VECLIB_MAXIMUM_THREADS = 1
)

# L'Ecuyer-CMRG gives workers reproducible independent streams.
# Matlab uses a different random-number stream.
RNGkind("L'Ecuyer-CMRG")
set.seed(1)
```

## 2. Helper functions

```{r functions}
# Within-demeaning is equivalent to country fixed effects and is faster here.
within_demean <- function(matrix_data, group, n_group) {
  group_sum <- matrix_data |>
    rowsum(group, reorder = FALSE)
  group_n <- group |>
    tabulate(nbins = n_group)
  group_mean <- group_sum |>
    sweep(1, group_n, "/")
  matrix_data - group_mean[group, , drop = FALSE]
}

multi_ols <- function(x_matrix, y_matrix) {
  qx <- x_matrix |>
    qr(tol = 1e-10)
  if (qx$rank < ncol(x_matrix)) {
    stop("The regression matrix is rank deficient.")
  }
  qx |>
    qr.coef(y_matrix)
}

fit_fe_var <- function(y_matrix, x_lag, group, n_group) {
  x_dm <- x_lag |>
    within_demean(group, n_group)
  y_dm <- y_matrix |>
    within_demean(group, n_group)
  beta <- x_dm |>
    multi_ols(y_dm)

  unexplained <- y_matrix - x_lag %*% beta
  group_n <- group |>
    tabulate(nbins = n_group)
  alpha <- unexplained |>
    rowsum(group, reorder = FALSE) |>
    sweep(1, group_n, "/")
  residuals <- unexplained - alpha[group, , drop = FALSE]

  list(beta = beta, alpha = alpha, residuals = residuals)
}

# Actual initial lags are retained to match the authors' conditional bootstrap.
simulate_panel <- function(beta, innovations, x_initial, group,
                           group_sizes, p, alpha = NULL) {
  n_obs <- nrow(x_initial)
  n_y <- ncol(beta)
  x_boot <- matrix(0, n_obs, n_y * p)
  y_boot <- matrix(0, n_obs, n_y)
  starts <- cumsum(c(1L, head(group_sizes, -1L)))

  for (i in seq_along(group_sizes)) {
    idx <- starts[i] + seq_len(group_sizes[i]) - 1L
    x_boot[idx[1], ] <- x_initial[idx[1], ]

    for (k in seq_along(idx)) {
      r <- idx[k]
      fixed_effect <- if (is.null(alpha)) 0 else alpha[i, ]
      y_boot[r, ] <- drop(x_boot[r, ] %*% beta) +
        fixed_effect + innovations[r, ]

      if (k < length(idx)) {
        x_boot[idx[k + 1L], ] <- c(
          y_boot[r, ],
          x_boot[r, seq_len(n_y * (p - 1L))]
        )
      }
    }
  }

  list(y = y_boot, x = x_boot)
}

cholesky_irf <- function(beta, sigma, p, horizon = 10L) {
  n_y <- ncol(beta)

  # Data order: household debt, firm debt, GDP.
  # Identification order: GDP, firm debt, household debt.
  permutation <- rbind(
    c(0, 0, 1),
    c(0, 1, 0),
    c(1, 0, 0)
  )
  impact <- t(permutation) %*%
    t(chol(permutation %*% sigma %*% t(permutation))) %*%
    permutation

  # The paper normalizes shocks to a one-unit own-variable impact response.
  impact <- sweep(impact, 2, diag(impact), "/")

  companion <- matrix(0, n_y * p, n_y * p)
  companion[seq_len(n_y), ] <- t(beta[seq_len(n_y * p), , drop = FALSE])
  if (p > 1L) {
    companion[
      (n_y + 1L):(n_y * p),
      seq_len(n_y * (p - 1L))
    ] <- diag(n_y * (p - 1L))
  }

  irf <- array(0, dim = c(horizon + 1L, n_y, n_y))
  transition <- diag(n_y * p)
  for (h in 0:horizon) {
    if (h > 0L) transition <- companion %*% transition
    irf[h + 1L, , ] <- transition[seq_len(n_y), seq_len(n_y)] %*% impact
  }
  irf
}

estimate_bias_corrected_beta <- function(y_matrix, x_lag, fit_ols, group,
                                         group_sizes, p, n_boot,
                                         tol, update_step, max_iter,
                                         workers) {
  n_y <- ncol(y_matrix)
  n_obs <- nrow(y_matrix)
  n_group <- length(group_sizes)

  # Matches Matlab's detrend(res,'constant').
  centered_res <- fit_ols$residuals |>
    scale(center = TRUE, scale = FALSE)
  beta_trial <- fit_ols$beta

  for (iteration in seq_len(max_iter)) {
    message(sprintf(
      "Bias-correction iteration %d: %d draws on %d workers",
      iteration,
      n_boot,
      workers
    ))

    beta_list <- seq_len(n_boot) |>
      parallel::mclapply(
        FUN = function(b) {
          # Observation-level signs reproduce the first-stage wild bootstrap.
          signs <- sample(c(-1, 1), n_obs, replace = TRUE)
          innovations_boot <- centered_res * signs

          simulation <- beta_trial |>
            simulate_panel(
              innovations_boot,
              x_lag,
              group,
              group_sizes,
              p,
              alpha = NULL
            )
          simulation$y |>
            fit_fe_var(simulation$x, group, n_group) |>
            getElement("beta")
        },
        mc.cores = workers,
        mc.preschedule = TRUE,
        mc.set.seed = TRUE
      )

    beta_draws <- beta_list |>
      unlist(use.names = FALSE) |>
      array(dim = c(n_y * p, n_y, n_boot))
    mean_beta_b <- beta_draws |>
      apply(c(1, 2), mean)
    difference <- fit_ols$beta - mean_beta_b
    convergence <- (difference^2) |>
      mean() |>
      sqrt()
    message(sprintf(
      "Bias-correction iteration %d: RMSE = %.6f",
      iteration, convergence
    ))

    if (convergence <= tol) break
    beta_trial <- beta_trial + update_step * difference
  }

  if (convergence > tol) {
    warning("Bias correction did not converge; increase n_boot_bias.")
  }

  list(
    beta = beta_trial,
    bias = fit_ols$beta - beta_trial,
    convergence = convergence,
    iterations = iteration
  )
}

bootstrap_irf_ci <- function(y_matrix, x_lag, year, group, group_sizes, p,
                             beta_bc, alpha_bc, residuals_bc,
                             estimated_bias, n_boot, horizon,
                             df_residual, workers) {
  n_y <- ncol(y_matrix)
  n_obs <- nrow(y_matrix)
  n_group <- length(group_sizes)
  all_years <- seq(min(year), max(year))

  # Matches the finite-sample residual scaling in the Matlab program.
  res_for_boot <- (residuals_bc * sqrt(n_obs / df_residual)) |>
    scale(center = TRUE, scale = FALSE)

  message(sprintf(
    "IRF confidence intervals: %d draws on %d workers",
    n_boot,
    workers
  ))

  irf_list <- seq_len(n_boot) |>
    parallel::mclapply(
      FUN = function(b) {
        # Common within-year signs preserve cross-country dependence.
        year_sign <- sample(c(-1, 1), length(all_years), replace = TRUE)
        signs <- year_sign[match(year, all_years)]
        innovations_boot <- res_for_boot * signs

        simulation <- beta_bc |>
          simulate_panel(
            innovations_boot,
            x_lag,
            group,
            group_sizes,
            p,
            alpha = alpha_bc
          )

        raw_fit <- simulation$y |>
          fit_fe_var(simulation$x, group, n_group)
        beta_boot_bc <- raw_fit$beta - estimated_bias

        # Matlab retains each draw's uncorrected fixed effects.
        residuals_boot <- simulation$y - simulation$x %*% beta_boot_bc -
          raw_fit$alpha[group, , drop = FALSE]
        sigma_boot <- crossprod(residuals_boot) / df_residual

        beta_boot_bc |>
          cholesky_irf(sigma_boot, p, horizon)
      },
      mc.cores = workers,
      mc.preschedule = TRUE,
      mc.set.seed = TRUE
    )

  irf_draws <- irf_list |>
    unlist(use.names = FALSE) |>
    array(dim = c(horizon + 1L, n_y, n_y, n_boot))
  message("IRF confidence-interval bootstrap completed.")

  list(
    lower = irf_draws |>
      apply(c(1, 2, 3), quantile, probs = 0.025, names = FALSE),
    upper = irf_draws |>
      apply(c(1, 2, 3), quantile, probs = 0.975, names = FALSE)
  )
}
```

## 3. Read the authors' prepared VAR data

```{r data, message=FALSE}
raw <- read_csv("figure1_data.csv", col_names = FALSE)

# Column positions are defined by the authors' headerless Matlab input.
country_id <- raw |>
  pull(1)
year <- raw |>
  pull(2)
y_matrix <- raw |>
  select(7:9) |>
  as.matrix()
x_lag <- raw |>
  select(10:24) |>
  as.matrix()

colnames(y_matrix) <- c("Household debt", "Firm debt", "Real GDP")

p <- 5L
horizon <- 10L
country_levels <- country_id |>
  unique()
group <- country_id |>
  match(country_levels)
group_sizes <- group |>
  tabulate(nbins = length(country_levels))
n_country <- country_levels |>
  length()
n_y <- y_matrix |>
  ncol()
n_obs <- y_matrix |>
  nrow()

n_parameters <- n_y * p + n_country
df_residual <- n_obs - n_parameters

stopifnot(n_country == 30L, ncol(x_lag) == n_y * p)
c(
  observations = n_obs, countries = n_country,
  first_year = min(year), last_year = max(year), workers = workers
)
```

## 4. Estimate and bias-correct the panel VAR

```{r estimation}
fit_ols <- y_matrix |>
  fit_fe_var(x_lag, group, n_country)

bias_result <- y_matrix |>
  estimate_bias_corrected_beta(
    x_lag = x_lag, fit_ols = fit_ols,
    group = group, group_sizes = group_sizes, p = p,
    n_boot = n_boot_bias, tol = bias_tol,
    update_step = bias_step, max_iter = max_bias_iterations,
    workers = workers
  )

beta_bc <- bias_result$beta

unexplained_bc <- y_matrix - x_lag %*% beta_bc
alpha_bc <- unexplained_bc |>
  rowsum(group, reorder = FALSE) |>
  sweep(1, group_sizes, "/")
residuals_bc <- unexplained_bc - alpha_bc[group, , drop = FALSE]
sigma_bc <- crossprod(residuals_bc) / df_residual

irf_point <- beta_bc |>
  cholesky_irf(sigma_bc, p, horizon)
```

## 5. Construct 95% confidence intervals

```{r bootstrap-ci}
irf_ci <- y_matrix |>
  bootstrap_irf_ci(
    x_lag = x_lag, year = year,
    group = group, group_sizes = group_sizes, p = p,
    beta_bc = beta_bc, alpha_bc = alpha_bc,
    residuals_bc = residuals_bc,
    estimated_bias = bias_result$bias,
    n_boot = n_boot_ci, horizon = horizon,
    df_residual = df_residual, workers = workers
  )
```

## 6. Figure I

```{r figure-1, fig.cap="Impulse Responses from a Recursive VAR in Real GDP, Nonfinancial Firm Debt, and Household Debt"}
make_panel_data <- function(response, shock) {
  tibble(
    year_after_impulse = 0:horizon,
    impulse_response = irf_point[, response, shock],
    lower_ci = irf_ci$lower[, response, shock],
    upper_ci = irf_ci$upper[, response, shock]
  )
}

draw_panel <- function(data, title, y_limits, y_breaks,
                       x_title = NULL, show_legend = FALSE) {
  panel <- data |>
    ggplot(aes(x = .data$year_after_impulse)) +
    geom_line(
      aes(y = .data$impulse_response, linetype = "Impulse response"),
      linewidth = 0.45
    ) +
    geom_point(aes(y = .data$impulse_response), size = 1) +
    geom_line(
      aes(y = .data$lower_ci, linetype = "95% CI"),
      linewidth = 0.45
    ) +
    geom_line(
      aes(y = .data$upper_ci, linetype = "95% CI"),
      linewidth = 0.45
    ) +
    scale_linetype_manual(
      values = c("Impulse response" = "solid", "95% CI" = "dashed"),
      breaks = c("Impulse response", "95% CI")
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 10, 2)) +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = y_breaks) +
    coord_cartesian(
      xlim = c(0, 10),
      ylim = y_limits,
      expand = FALSE
    ) +
    labs(title = title, x = x_title, y = NULL, linetype = NULL) +
    theme_bw(base_family = "serif", base_size = 10) +
    theme(
      panel.grid = element_blank(),
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
      legend.key.width = grid::unit(1.2, "lines"),
      legend.background = element_rect(colour = "black")
    )

  if (show_legend) {
    panel + theme(
      legend.position = "inside",
      legend.position.inside = c(0.22, 0.14)
    )
  } else {
    panel + theme(legend.position = "none")
  }
}

# IRF array order is [horizon, response, shock].
panel_hh_hh <- make_panel_data(response = 1, shock = 1) |>
  draw_panel(
    title = expression(d^"HH" %->% d^"HH"),
    y_limits = c(-0.2, 1.8),
    y_breaks = seq(0, 1.5, 0.5),
    show_legend = TRUE
  )

panel_hh_gdp <- make_panel_data(response = 3, shock = 1) |>
  draw_panel(
    title = expression(d^"HH" %->% y),
    y_limits = c(-0.8, 0.6),
    y_breaks = seq(-0.8, 0.6, 0.2),
    x_title = "Years after impulse"
  )

panel_f_gdp <- make_panel_data(response = 3, shock = 2) |>
  draw_panel(
    title = expression(d^"F" %->% y),
    y_limits = c(-0.5, 0.4),
    y_breaks = seq(-0.4, 0.4, 0.2)
  )

figure_1 <- panel_hh_hh + panel_hh_gdp + panel_f_gdp
figure_1
```
